title: Rick Hoberg

'''Rick Hoberg''' is a comic-book artist from Belton, Texas. He inked a few [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel ''Star Wars'']] issues and also did some covers for the title.

He started in the comics industry in the mid 1970s assisting [[Russ Manning]] on the early ''[[Star Wars]]'' and [[Wikipedia:Tarzan|''Tarzan'']] [[Star Wars (comic strip)|newspaper strips]]. In the 1980s he worked on several [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC Comics]] including [[Wikipedia:The Brave and the Bold|''The Brave and the Bold'']] and [[Wikipedia:Batman|''Batman'']].

He has also done production sketches and merchandise work for [[Lucasfilm]], including drawings for a number of versions of [[The Empire Strikes Back Coloring Book (1980)|''The Empire Strikes Back Coloring Book'']]. Hoberg has also worked in animation for such companies as [[Wikipedia:Hanna-Barbera|Hanna-Barbera]] and [[Wikipedia:DiC Entertainment|DiC Entertainment]].
